data.title: Binance Sets Alpha Points Threshold for River’s BuildKey TGE: 209 Credits for Phase 1
data.description: Key Points:Binance announced eligibility requirements for River’s BuildKey Token Generation Event (TGE).Phase 1 (Subscription): Requires 209 Alpha Points, with 15 points deducted upon participation.Phase 2 (Trading): Requires 61 Alpha Points, with no deduction.Binance Confirms BuildKey TGE Entry RulesBinance announced on its official X platform that users must meet specific Alpha Points thresholds to take part in the first phases of River’s BuildKey TGE.Phase 1 – BuildKey Subscription: Users need at least 209 Alpha Points. Participation will also deduct 15 points from their balance.Phase 2 – BuildKey Trading: Users need at least 61 Alpha Points, but no points will be deducted for joining.Alpha Points as a Gateway to New ProjectsAlpha Points act as Binance’s eligibility credits for early access to new project launches, airdrops, and token generation events. The BuildKey TGE is one of the latest opportunities for eligible users to secure allocations before trading begins.Binance noted that further details on the BuildKey TGE phases, including timelines and allocations, will be shared separately.

CZ, in a post on the X platform, mentioned that TWT's token price had previously increased after modifying its token economic model, and stated that TWT's FDV increased after the token was destroyed.

data.title: Why we Bitcoin — Vietnam closes 86M bank accounts that fail biometrics
data.description:
Vietnam is reportedly closing 86 million bank accounts. Crypto advocates see it as the latest reason everyone should hold Bitcoin.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/bitcoiners-criticize-vietnam-closing-86m-bank-accounts?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Vietnam's Biometric Banking Mandate Sparks Bitcoin Advocacy
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, Vietnam has initiated the closure of over 86 million bank accounts that did not comply with a new facial biometric authentication requirement. This move, reported by several Vietnamese media outlets in July, began on September 1. The remaining 113 million bank accounts have been verified under the new biometric laws, aimed at preventing fraud and money laundering. A Reddit user, "Yukzor," who is a former foreign contractor in Vietnam, shared his experience of having to return to the country to prevent his HSBC bank account from being closed, as there was no remote solution available. He expressed frustration over the necessity to travel in person to resolve such issues in 2025.

Bitcoin advocates have long argued for the importance of having access to personal funds without government or external interference. Marty Bent, a Bitcoin industry commentator, highlighted the situation, stating that if users do not comply by September 30, they risk losing their money. Bent emphasized that such scenarios underscore the need for Bitcoin. Cointelegraph could not verify whether customer funds would indeed be unrecoverable after the deadline. Bent also noted that similar capital controls have occurred in countries like Lebanon, Turkey, Venezuela, Cyprus, Nigeria, and India since Bitcoin's inception, suggesting that Vietnam may not be the last to implement such measures. Daniel Batten, a Bitcoin environmentalist, commented that the strict measure provides Vietnam's central bank with advanced financial surveillance capabilities, reinforcing the necessity of permissionless monetary protocols like Bitcoin to protect against state overreach.

Vietnam's introduction of these measures follows a rise in generative AI and sophisticated spoofing techniques that have bypassed security measures like liveness detection in recent years. In May, local authorities dismantled an AI-powered money laundering operation that used fake facial scans to launder approximately 1 trillion Vietnamese dong ($39 million). To comply with the new regulations, bank customers must complete an initial facial biometric authentication and repeat the process for online transfers exceeding 10 million Vietnamese dong ($379), as stated by the State Bank of Vietnam in late June. However, a crypto executive based in Vietnam suggested that the impact of these changes might be overstated, noting that most locals have not been significantly affected. The changes primarily impact foreign residents with inactive accounts. Herbert Sim, AICEAN's chief marketing officer, mentioned that the requirement for in-person biometric verification poses significant challenges, particularly for foreigners who have left the country or have inactive accounts.

data.title: Wall Street's Blockchain Initiative Aims to Boost Productivity
data.description: According to Foresight News, Tom Lee, Chairman of BitMine's Board, announced via a tweet that Wall Street is taking steps to enhance productivity by building on blockchain technology.

data.title: 5-year and 10-year Japanese government bond yields climb to their highest level since 2008
data.description: Japan's 5-year government bond yield climbed to its highest point since 2008; the 10-year Japanese government bond yield rose 4.5 basis points to 1.2%, the highest level since October 2008. (Jinshi)

data.title: Binance will adjust margin requirements for PM Pro and futures contracts
data.description: According to an official announcement, Binance will adjust the collateralization ratio and tiered collateralization ratio for PMPro in the Portfolio Margin model at 2:00 PM Beijing Time on September 23, 2025, and 2:00 PM Beijing Time on September 26, 2025. This adjustment will affect multiple asset classes, including PUMP and LINEA. Additionally, Binance Futures will adjust the leverage and margin tiers for several U-margined perpetual contracts, including ETHFIUSDT and ATHUSDT, starting at 2:30 PM Beijing Time on September 20, 2025. (This newsletter was generated with AI assistance.)

data.title: Boyaa Interactive: Will further explore Web3 ecological business cooperation based on compliance framework with Xinhuo Technology
data.description: According to a Hong Kong announcement by Boyaa Interactive (00434.HK), the company established a strategic partnership with Newfire Technology Holdings Limited in September 2024. The Group will further explore business collaboration within the Web 3 ecosystem based on a compliance framework with Newfire Technology to jointly explore development opportunities within the Web 3 ecosystem. Newfire Technology will provide the Group with digital asset compliance management plans and Web 3-related technical solutions. The Group's Board of Directors believes that this strategic partnership will play a significant supporting role in the implementation of the Group's Web 3 strategic transformation, and that Newfire Technology's technical expertise and industry resources will further promote the Group's business development and layout in the Web 3 sector. (Jinshi)

data.title: Analysts: The Bank of Japan's interest rate decision was unexpected and may see a rate hike in October
data.description: Hirofumi Suzuki, an analyst at Sumitomo Mitsui Banking Corporation, said the result was unexpected. While the market generally expected the Bank of Japan to simply maintain its policy unchanged, the launch of the ETF sales program and the dissenting votes of two members against maintaining policy (in favor of a 25 basis point rate hike) gave the meeting a hawkish tone. The timeline suggests that even with the LDP leadership election on October 4th, the Bank of Japan is signaling a steady pace of policy normalization. Another rate hike is expected in October. (Jinshi)

data.title: Binance Launches Solayer (LAYER) Fourth Round of HODLer Airdrops
data.description: Binance has officially announced that it will launch the fourth round of HODLer airdrops as part of its 18,000,000 LAYER airdrop program. Details of the fourth round of LAYER HODLer airdrops: 6,000,000 LAYER will be distributed in this fourth round of HODLer airdrops. Eligible users: Users who subscribe to guaranteed principal earning (fixed and/or demand) or on-chain earning products using BNB between 08:00 on September 12, 2025, and 07:59 on September 15, 2025 (GMT+8) will receive the airdrop. Distribution will be completed within 24 hours of this announcement.

data.title: This week's mining data highlights: Bitcoin hits a high of $117,998, while the network's computing power drops 5.24%.
data.description: Week 38, 2025 (September 13-19): 1. According to cloverpool, the average hash rate of the Bitcoin network was 991 EH/s, with a high of 1102 EH/s and a low of 812 EH/s, a decrease of 5.24% from the previous week's average hash rate of 1046 EH/s. 2. According to blockchain.com, the average price of Bitcoin was $115,947, with a high of $117,998 and a low of $114,396, a 4.01% increase from the previous week's average price of $111,481. 3. Mining news worth noting include: (1) Delin Holdings plans to acquire 2,200 Bitcoin mining machines from Fortune Peak; (2) Nvidia will invest 500 million pounds in AI infrastructure company Nscale, a spin-off of a crypto mining company; (3) Bitmain accuses Orb Energy of misappropriating Bitcoin and damaging mining machines; Data partner: Cango Inc. (CANG), a Bitcoin mining company listed on the New York Stock Exchange.

data.title: Coinbase integrates Morpho to launch USDC interest-earning feature, with yields up to 10.8%
data.description: According to Cointelegraph, Coinbase has launched a new feature by integrating Morpho, allowing users to directly participate in on-chain DeFi lending through its app and earn USDC income, with an annualized yield of up to 10.8%.

data.title: Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $213 million yesterday, while none of the nine ETFs had a net outflow
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $213 million yesterday (September 18th, US Eastern Time). The Fidelity ETF (FETH) saw the largest single-day net inflow yesterday, with a net inflow of $159 million, bringing FETH's total net inflow to $2.927 billion. The next largest daily inflow was the Grayscale Ethereum Mini Trust ETF (ETH), with a net inflow of $22.9041 million, bringing ETH's total net inflow to $1.454 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Ethereum spot ETFs was $30.539 billion, with a net asset value ratio (market capitalization relative to Ethereum's total market capitalization) of 5.49%, and cumulative net inflows to $13.872 billion.

data.title: Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $163 million yesterday, with no net outflow among the twelve ETFs.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $163 million yesterday (September 18th, US Eastern Time). The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest single-day net inflow yesterday was the Fidelity FBTC ETF, which saw a net inflow of $97.3526 million, bringing FBTC's total net inflow to $12.661 billion. The next largest inflow was the Ark Invest and 21 Shares ETF, ARKB, with a net inflow of $24.9951 million, bringing ARKB's total net inflow to $2.227 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs was $155.053 billion, with a net asset value ratio (market capitalization relative to the total Bitcoin market capitalization) of 6.62%, and cumulative net inflows reaching $57.495 billion.

data.title: Bybit becomes the first cryptocurrency exchange to support QCDT mirror staking
data.description: Bybit announced a strategic partnership with Qatar National Bank (QNB Group) and DMZ Finance to launch QCDT, the world's first tokenized money market fund approved by the Dubai Financial Services Authority (DFSA), as a mirror collateral asset on the Bybit platform. As the first cryptocurrency exchange to support QCDT as collateral, Bybit unlocks up to $1 billion in lending capacity for institutional investors, setting a new benchmark for the integration of real-world assets (RWAs) and digital finance. QCDT uses U.S. Treasury bonds as its underlying asset, with tokenization technology provided by DMZ Finance, management by QNB Group, and custody by Standard Chartered Bank ensuring compliance, transparency, and security. Bybit stated that this move not only provides established trading institutions and traditional financial institutions with a low-risk, secure, and compliant way to participate in digital assets, but also further solidifies its position as a bridge between traditional finance and the crypto economy in the Middle East and globally.

data.title: China skips U.S. soybeans to start season, reviving trade war tactic
data.description:
China has not booked a single shipment of U. S. soybeans since the new export season began, breaking a buying pattern that’s held firm since at least 1999. This is not some random slowdown, though; it’s a repeat of a trade war tactic first used under Donald Trump. As of September 11, nearly two weeks into […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/china-skips-u-s-soybeans-to-start-season/
